sect4.title: AAP Pediatric Coding Newsletter
sect4.para: "Did You Know? The CLIA-Waived Test Modifier," February 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/peco book238 document003)

sect4.title: CLIA Test Categorization
sect4.para: FDA CLIA database (www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfCLIA/search.cfm) "Provider-Performed Microscopy Procedures" from the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (www.cms.gov/regulations-and-guidance/legislation/clia/downloads/ppmplist.pdf)

sect4.title: HIPAA Release of Patient Records
sect4.para: "Questions and Answers About HIPAA's Access Right" (45 CFR §164.524) (www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/guidance/access/index.html#newlyreleasedFaqs)

sect4.title: Medicare Physician Fee Schedule
sect4.para: How to Use the PFS Look-Up Tool booklet (www.cms.gov/sites/default/files/2021-04/How to MPFS Booklet ICN901344_0.pdf)

sect4.title: National Drug Codes
sect4.para: FDA National Drug Code Directory (www.fda.gov/drugs/drug-approvals-and-databases/national-drug-code-directory) ● indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✱, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

blockquote.para: 1. What modifier is appended to code 93010 (routine ECG with at least 12 leads; interpretation and report only) when a physician provides only the interpretation and report of the ECG?
c. None; no modifier is necessary according to the code descriptor.
2. Which type of specimen collection is separately reported?
3. Which service is reported with modifier QW?
a. A laboratory service reported by a physician or QHP but provided by another entity > b. All laboratory services provided in an office or outpatient setting
c. Collection of a specimen for laboratory testing > d. Performance of a laboratory test that is CLIA-waived (with a few exceptions)
4. For which testing platform is code selection based on the analysis performed and the proprietary name of the test?
5. Which of the following is true when reporting an E/M service and testing services on the same date?
a. The physician's or QHP's analysis of test results that do not require interpretation and report is part of an evaluation and management (E/M) service.
b. Modifier 25 may be required on the E/M service to indicate the service was significant and is separately identifiable in the documentation.
c. Time spent providing a separately reported testing service does not contribute to the total time of an E/M service.

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; +, add-on; ✱, modifier 51 exempt; ⚡, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.
